speaker: TROILUS
text: Dear, trouble not yourself. The morn is cold.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Then, sweet my lord, I’ll call mine uncle down.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: He shall unbolt the gates.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Trouble him not.

speaker: TROILUS
text: To bed, to bed! Sleep kill those pretty eyes

speaker: TROILUS
text: And give as soft attachment to thy senses

speaker: TROILUS
text: As infants’ empty of all thought!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Good morrow, then.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I prithee now, to bed.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Are you aweary of me?

speaker: TROILUS
text: O Cressida! But that the busy day,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Waked by the lark, hath roused the ribald crows,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And dreaming night will hide our joys no longer,

speaker: TROILUS
text: I would not from thee.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Night hath been too brief.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Beshrew the witch! With venomous wights she stays

speaker: TROILUS
text: As tediously as hell, but flies the grasps of love

speaker: TROILUS
text: With wings more momentary-swift than thought.

speaker: TROILUS
text: You will catch cold and curse me.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Prithee, tarry. You men will never tarry.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: O foolish Cressid! I might have still held off,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And then you would have tarried. Hark, there’s one up.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: What’s all the doors open here?

speaker: TROILUS
text: It is your uncle.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: A pestilence on him! Now will he be mocking.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I shall have such a life!

speaker: PANDARUS
text: How now, how now? How go maidenheads?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Here, you maid! Where’s my Cousin Cressid?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Go hang yourself, you naughty mocking uncle.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: You bring me to do—and then you flout me too.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: To do what, to do what?—Let her say

speaker: PANDARUS
text: what.—What have I brought you to do?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Come, come, beshrew your heart! You’ll ne’er be good

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Nor suffer others.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Ha, ha! Alas, poor wretch! Ah, poor capocchia!

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Has ’t not slept tonight? Would he not—a

speaker: PANDARUS
text: naughty man—let it sleep? A bugbear take him!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Did not I tell you? Would he were knocked i’ th’ head!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Who’s that at door?—Good uncle, go and see.—

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: My lord, come you again into my chamber.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: You smile and mock me, as if I meant naughtily.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Ha, ha!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Come, you are deceived. I think of no such thing.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: How earnestly they knock! Pray you, come in.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I would not for half Troy have you seen here.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Who’s there? What’s the matter? Will you

speaker: PANDARUS
text: beat down the door?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: How now? What’s the matter?

speaker: AENEAS
text: Good morrow, lord, good morrow.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Who’s there? My Lord Aeneas? By my troth,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: I knew you not. What news with you so early?

speaker: AENEAS
text: Is not Prince Troilus here?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Here? What should he do here?

speaker: AENEAS
text: Come, he is here, my lord. Do not deny him.

speaker: AENEAS
text: It doth import him much to speak with me.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Is he here, say you? It’s more than I know,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: I’ll be sworn. For my own part, I came in late.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: What should he do here?

speaker: AENEAS
text: Ho, nay, then! Come, come, you’ll do him

speaker: AENEAS
text: wrong ere you are ware. You’ll be so true to him to

speaker: AENEAS
text: be false to him. Do not you know of him, but yet go

speaker: AENEAS
text: fetch him hither. Go.

speaker: TROILUS
text: How now? What’s the matter?

speaker: AENEAS
text: My lord, I scarce have leisure to salute you,

speaker: AENEAS
text: My matter is so rash. There is at hand

speaker: AENEAS
text: Paris your brother and Deiphobus,

speaker: AENEAS
text: The Grecian Diomed, and our Antenor

speaker: AENEAS
text: Delivered to us; and for him forthwith,

speaker: AENEAS
text: Ere the first sacrifice, within this hour,

speaker: AENEAS
text: We must give up to Diomedes’ hand

speaker: AENEAS
text: The Lady Cressida.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Is it so concluded?

speaker: AENEAS
text: By Priam and the general state of Troy.

speaker: AENEAS
text: They are at hand and ready to effect it.

speaker: TROILUS
text: How my achievements mock me!

speaker: TROILUS
text: I will go meet them. And, my Lord Aeneas,

speaker: TROILUS
text: We met by chance; you did not find me here.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Good, good, my lord; the secrets of nature

speaker: AENEAS
text: Have not more gift in taciturnity.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Is ’t possible? No sooner got but lost? The

speaker: PANDARUS
text: devil take Antenor! The young prince will go mad.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: A plague upon Antenor! I would they had broke ’s

speaker: PANDARUS
text: neck!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: How now? What’s the matter? Who was here?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Ah, ah!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Why sigh you so profoundly? Where’s my lord?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Gone? Tell me, sweet uncle, what’s the matter?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Would I were as deep under the earth as I

speaker: PANDARUS
text: am above!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: O the gods! What’s the matter?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Pray thee, get thee in. Would thou hadst

speaker: PANDARUS
text: ne’er been born! I knew thou wouldst be his death.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: O, poor gentleman! A plague upon Antenor!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Good uncle, I beseech you, on my knees I

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: beseech you, what’s the matter?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Thou must be gone, wench; thou must be

speaker: PANDARUS
text: gone. Thou art changed for Antenor. Thou must to

speaker: PANDARUS
text: thy father and be gone from Troilus. ’Twill be his

speaker: PANDARUS
text: death; ’twill be his bane. He cannot bear it.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: O you immortal gods! I will not go.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Thou must.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I will not, uncle. I have forgot my father.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I know no touch of consanguinity,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: No kin, no love, no blood, no soul so near me

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: As the sweet Troilus. O you gods divine,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Make Cressid’s name the very crown of falsehood

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: If ever she leave Troilus! Time, force, and death

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Do to this body what extremes you can,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: But the strong base and building of my love

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Is as the very center of the Earth,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Drawing all things to it. I’ll go in and weep—

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Do, do.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Tear my bright hair, and scratch my praisèd cheeks,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Crack my clear voice with sobs, and break my heart

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: With sounding “Troilus.” I will not go from Troy.
